diagnosis: A large, conspicuous ground beetle of striking metallic coloration. Distinguished from sympatric Calosoma by the combination of brilliant green elytra with coppery marginal reflections, deep blue-violet pronotum, and large body size. Readily flies; often attracted to lights and to outbreaks of caterpillar prey.

diagnostic_characters.label: Elytral color
diagnostic_characters.note: With coppery, gold, or reddish reflections along the lateral and apical margins.

diagnostic_characters.label: Pronotum
diagnostic_characters.note: Margins broadly explanate and reflexed; widest near anterior third.

diagnostic_characters.label: Elytral sculpture
diagnostic_characters.note: Intervals nearly flat with three rows of small ocellate setigerous pits.

images.caption: Dorsal habitus, female. Tippecanoe Co., IN. 14 June 2024.

similar_species_notes.calosoma_sycophanta: Smaller (21–28 mm); elytra brighter green with no marginal red.
similar_species_notes.calosoma_calidum: Smaller, dull black elytra with three rows of golden pits.

references: Bousquet, Y. 2012. *Catalogue of Geadephaga (Coleoptera, Adephaga) of America, north of Mexico.* ZooKeys 245: 1–1722.
Lindroth, C. H. 1961. *The ground-beetles (Carabidae, excl. Cicindelinae) of Canada and Alaska.* Opuscula Entomologica, Supplementum 20.
Larochelle, A. & Larivière, M.-C. 2003. *A natural history of the ground-beetles (Coleoptera: Carabidae) of America north of Mexico.* Pensoft Publishers, Sofia.
Erwin, T. L. 2007. *A treatise on the western hemisphere Caraboidea (Coleoptera). Their classification, distributions, and ways of life.* Volume I. Pensoft, Sofia.
Ciegler, J. C. 2000. *Ground beetles and wrinkled bark beetles of South Carolina.* Biota of South Carolina, vol. 1.
